---
title: "Get Involved"
---

## Weekly Meetings

We hold weekly lab meetings open to anyone interested. Come by, see what we're working on, and meet the team — no commitment required.

**Location:** [Green Hall, GR 3.316](https://map.concept3d.com/?id=1772#!m/550863?share){target="_blank"} (Thomas Spencer Hall)

## Student Research Opportunities

PRISE Lab offers hands-on research experience for undergraduate and graduate students at UT Dallas. No prior research experience is required — just genuine interest and reliability. We mentor students through every step of the research process.

### New Projects

**Campus Drinking Water Quality Study** (Green Fund, Fall 2026)

We are assembling a student team to test drinking water quality across campus for PFAS, lead, and other contaminants, survey the campus community on willingness to pay for improvements, and conduct a cost-benefit analysis of treatment options. We need students from three areas:

- **Environmental Science** — field sampling, lab coordination, data interpretation
- **Social Science / Economics / Public Policy** — survey design, econometric analysis
- **Engineering** — water treatment costing, cost-benefit analysis

To apply, email [erin.litzow@utdallas.edu](mailto:erin.litzow@utdallas.edu) and [elias.cisneros@utdallas.edu](mailto:elias.cisneros@utdallas.edu) with your name, major, year, which team interests you, and why.

### Ongoing Projects

We also have openings on our ongoing research. If any of these topics interest you, reach out — we can match you to a project that fits your skills and goals.

- **Climate and political accountability** — How do extreme weather events shape election outcomes and climate policy?
- **Conservation program effectiveness** — Optimizing conservation funding using quasi-experimental methods
- **Clean energy adoption** — Household adoption of energy-efficient cooking appliances in developing countries
- **Land-use change and deforestation** — Remote sensing and causal analysis of deforestation in Brazil and Indonesia

### What You Get

- Real research experience for grad school apps, job interviews, and fellowships
- Hands-on training in field methods, survey design, economic valuation, or data analysis
- Co-authorship on reports and policy briefs
- Mentorship from faculty with active research programs

### Contact

Interested? Reach out to either faculty member on our [Team](team.qmd) page.
